Diagnosis_Remarks: Since 1997,patient has been continuously treated with Lamivudine 100 to 150 mg per day because of chronic hepatitis B (HBe-antigen negative mutant). Under this treatment, the aminotransferases normalized and the HBV DNA dropped to just above the limit of detection. In the 2nd half of 1999, however, aminotransferases increased again and the HBV DNA increased to more than 106 HBV copies/ml: this is probably due to the appearance of a YMDD mutant, as is more the case with long-term treatment with lamivudine. Due to exacerbation of liver disease, Interferon was still associated with Lamivudine 200 mg per day from February 2000 until the end of January 2001. Under this treatment, the aminotransferases improved slightly, but the HBV DNA remained very markedly elevated. At the last control under this treatment, the AST was normal (37 U/l), the ALT remained slightly elevated (56 U/l). Since stopping the Interferon, the patient feels better.  YMDD mutant after taking Zeffix.  Topical therapy with Hepsera, however, without complete suppression of the virus, but normalization of liver function parameters.

Diagnosis_Remarks: 1. Permanently impaired liver tests with mainly elevated ALT. Since last summer, the patient has gained 20 kg in weight.   Ultrasound: steatosis.   Elastometry:  not significant fibrosis, F0.  Blood: negative for viral and autoimmune disorders.   Metabolic, we see a slightly decreased ceruloplasmin, which does not explain the liver set abnormalities. In addition, there is also a strongly increased fasting triglyceridemia, for which a fibrate was started with beneficial effect. Lysosomal acid lipase is also negative. There is no further improvement in the liver set, mainly with an impaired ALT of 276 U/l. Normal bilirubin. No additional weight loss was achieved. No bile duct abnormalities were seen on MRCP, only steatosis with suspicion of a single vascular shunt in the right hepatic lobe. In view of no improvement of the liver set, a liver biopsy was performed, with an image of steatohepatitis, without signs of fibrosis (cave only small biopsy). The patient was again stimulated to lose weight. He was advised to do this in consultation with a dietician.    2. Known chronic atrophic gastritis with extensive lymphoid aggregates with past doubts about the diagnosis of MALT lymphoma. H. pylori eradication in 3 lines (biopsies after 2 lines negative, but H. pylori culture positive). Control biopsies afterwards negative for H. Pylori, showing chronic atrophic gastritis with moderately dense lymphoplasmocytic infiltrates, polyclonal, insufficient arguments for lymphoma. Control after 6 months was recent and showed an unchanged picture, insufficient arguments for MALT and negative staining for H. Pylori. No further gastroscopic follow-up required.    3. Delayed gastric emptying, for which treatment with Litican was initiated. In the past little effect of Motilium.
